text:
WWE legend and Olympic gold medalist Kurt Angle sent a congratulatory message to Cuban phenomenon Mijain Lopez after he made history with a gold at five straight Olympic Games.

The 41-year-old topped the podium yet again on Tuesday inParis, becoming the first athlete in Olympic history to win gold at five different Games in the same individual event.

The man known as The Giant of Herradura has won gold in the Greco-Roman wrestling at Beijing,London, Rio, Tokyo and now Paris at 130kg after dominating Chile’s Yasmani Acosta in the gold medal match.

Lopez turns 42 later this month and he has decided that the time has come to retire, ceremonially leaving his shoes on the mat after beating Acosta.

He bows out at the top of his sport and another man who won Olympic gold on the wrestling mat can scarcely believe what he has done.

1996 gold medalist Angle, who went on to achieve a legendary career in professional wrestling with WWE, hailed the Cuban for his historic success.

‘I usually don’t discuss athletes outside the USA but this guy did something nobody in history ever did,’ Angle posted on X. ‘He’s won 5 gold medals in 5 differentOlympics. Mijain Lopez!!! 41 years of age and STILL the best wrestler in the world. #greco #freestyle #wrestling.’

I usually donât discuss athletes outside the USA but this guy did something nobody in history ever did. Heâs won 5 gold medals in 5 different Olympics. Mijain Lopez!!! 41 years of age and STILL the best wrestler in the world.#greco#freestyle#wrestlingpic.twitter.com/Ehh5KcdsXu

The 6’5″ Lopez confirmed that he will be sailing off into the sunset after his quintet of gold medals, sending a message to the younger generation as he does.

‘Winning one medal in an Olympic game is not easy; winning three, four, or five is phenomenal,’ he said.

‘I think it’s time to say goodbye, to allow the young people who are inspired by sport and I just wanted to wish them success.’

Lopez has put himself in the discussion for the greatest Olympian of all-time with his unique achievements across five Games.

Before his campaign in Paris he told Olympics.com: ‘I honestly think I have not changed at all. I’m still the same Mijain, a simple and humble person.

‘That’s one of the things that sport has given me. I think that is what has led me to greatness in my sport. And that’s why all my fans, all my friends follow my journey because they know that Mijain Lopez is an athlete who gives everything to his sport, who not only fights for a medal for himself, but for everyone.

‘Mijain has not changed at all. It is still the same Mijain.’

Lopez said ahead of Tuesday’s final: ‘I know that in Cuba, everyone is in front of the television. There is only one step left to dance a little more.

‘I am happy to be able to fight here for my fifth gold medal and to be able to give the Cuban people joy.’

title: WWE legend sends message to record-breaking Olympic wrestler after historic gold | Metro News
description: WWE legend medalist Kurt Angle sent a message to Cuban phenomenon Mijain Lopez after he made history with a gold at five straight Olympic Games.
